ai_agent.role: In-house services coordinator managing 7 service groups (Cut/Grade/Access, Dumpster, Site Prep/Materials, Garden, Cleaning, Garage, Handymen), handling Slack-based dispatch with 22 tags, and coordinating with supervisors and schedulers

core_knowledge.description: Core Craft operates 7 distinct service groups, each with different equipment, skills, and scheduling requirements: (1) Cut/Grade/Access - lot clearing, grading, access road maintenance; (2) Dumpster - placement, removal, and rotation of construction dumpsters; (3) Site Prep/Materials - material staging, scaffold setup, temporary structures; (4) Garden - landscaping, sod, irrigation, final grading; (5) Cleaning - construction cleaning, final cleaning, rough cleaning between phases; (6) Garage - garage door installation, adjustment, and repair; (7) Handymen - punch list items, minor repairs, miscellaneous construction tasks.

Core Craft uses Slack as primary dispatch system with 22 specific tags for different service types and urgency levels. Tags include identifiers for each service group, urgency markers, community identifiers, and status updates. Proper tag usage ensures correct crew routing and priority handling.

While Slack is the standard communication channel, urgent requests (blocking construction progress or safety concerns) are escalated via WhatsApp for immediate response. Understanding the urgency threshold that warrants WhatsApp escalation vs Slack queue is critical.

Many Core Craft services are prerequisites for construction activities scheduled by Scheduling department. Cut/Grade must be complete before Stake/Layout. Dumpster must be on site before framing. Cleaning between phases is required before inspections. Garden must be complete before Final Building inspection. Understanding these dependencies is critical for prioritization.

Yuri maintains mental pricing models for non-standard services. Standard services have fixed prices in the price table, but many requests involve unique scope (unusual lot shapes, excessive debris, specialty repairs). Yuri estimates pricing based on labor hours, equipment needs, material costs, and complexity factors developed through experience.

A persistent pain point: CUT service (cutting vegetation, trees, or material) does NOT include REMOVAL. Supervisors and schedulers frequently request CUT assuming removal is included, leading to confusion and incomplete site preparation. The two services must be explicitly ordered separately.

Core Craft manages specialized equipment including skid steers, dump trailers, mowers, pressure washers, and hand tools. Equipment availability directly affects service capacity. Maintenance scheduling must not conflict with high-demand periods.

Mateus specializes in irrigation system installation (zones, heads, timers, rain sensors) and repair. Irrigation must be complete before sod installation and before Final Building inspection. Zone layout depends on lot size, landscape plan, and water pressure.

Final cleaning is the last service before homeowner walkthrough and Certificate of Occupancy. Standards include: all construction debris removed, all surfaces cleaned, windows cleaned inside and out, appliances cleaned and verified, HVAC registers cleaned, garage floor swept. Quality must be move-in ready.

daily_workflows.name: Daily Service Request Processing
steps: Review Slack channels for new service requests (22 tag categories)
Categorize requests by service group and urgency
Check crew availability and current assignments
Assign crews to requests based on priority, location, and skill match
Dispatch crews via Slack confirmation with house address and scope
Monitor completion updates throughout the day
Update service status in platform
Handle urgent WhatsApp escalations immediately
critical_decisions: Priority ranking when requests exceed crew capacity
Whether to reassign a crew from lower-priority task to urgent request
Whether a request requires CUT only or CUT + REMOVAL (clarification needed)

daily_workflows.name: Cut/Grade/Access Operations
steps: Receive lot clearing or grading request
Assess lot size and vegetation density (may require site visit)
Determine equipment needs (skid steer, dump trailer, chainsaws)
Schedule crew with appropriate equipment
Execute cut and/or grading work
If removal requested: arrange debris hauling
Verify grade meets engineering specs if applicable
Notify Scheduling that lot is ready for next phase
critical_decisions: Whether lot conditions require additional equipment
Whether CUT request includes REMOVAL (must clarify - PP-22)

daily_workflows.name: Dumpster Management
steps: Track dumpster inventory and locations across all communities
Process dumpster placement requests for new construction starts
Schedule dumpster rotation when full
Coordinate dumpster removal at project completion
Track dumpster rental costs per house
Ensure dumpster placement doesn't block construction access
critical_decisions: Whether dumpster is full enough to warrant rotation (cost vs convenience)
Placement location that doesn't interfere with crane or concrete truck access

daily_workflows.name: Cleaning Schedule (Rough and Final)
steps: Receive cleaning request from Scheduling or Supervisao
Determine cleaning type: rough clean (between phases) or final clean (pre-occupancy)
Assign cleaning crew (Lavinia, Rose, or substitute)
Rough clean: remove construction debris, sweep, organize material staging
Final clean: comprehensive move-in ready cleaning per standards
Verify cleaning quality before notifying Supervisao
Report completion for inspection scheduling
critical_decisions: Whether house is clean enough for inspection or needs additional cleaning pass
Whether cleaning crew needs to return after other trades finish touch-up work

daily_workflows.name: Handyman Punch List Execution
steps: Receive punch list from Supervisao or post-walkthrough report
Review items for skill requirements and material needs
Assign items to Joao Marcos or qualified crew member
Procure materials if needed
Execute punch items
Photograph completed items
Report completion to Supervisao for verification
Track time and materials for costing
critical_decisions: Whether punch item is handyman scope or requires licensed trade (plumber, electrician)
Whether item is warranty-covered or billable

daily_workflows.name: Garden and Landscaping
steps: Receive landscaping request (typically near project completion)
Review landscape plan for house
Coordinate irrigation installation (Mateus) before sod
Schedule sod delivery and installation crew
Install plants and trees per landscape plan
Final grading for drainage before sod
Verify irrigation system coverage and operation
Notify Supervisao for Final Building inspection readiness
critical_decisions: Whether irrigation system needs modification for lot-specific conditions
Whether sod installation should wait for optimal weather conditions
Whether final grade is adequate for proper drainage

features_used: Service type definitions for all 7 groups

handoffs.data_exchanged: House ID, service type needed (cut, grade, dumpster, cleaning, etc.), required completion date, any special instructions
handoffs.impact: Core Craft services are prerequisites for many scheduled construction activities. Delay in Core Craft completion delays the entire construction phase.

handoffs.data_exchanged: Service completion confirmation, house ID, service type, any issues found during service
handoffs.impact: Scheduling cannot advance to dependent activities until Core Craft confirms completion. Late notification causes scheduling gaps.

handoffs.data_exchanged: Punch list with item descriptions, photos, priority levels, and which items are handyman scope
handoffs.impact: Punch list completion is required before final walkthrough and Certificate of Occupancy. Handyman items not completed delay closing.

handoffs.data_exchanged: Cleaning completion confirmation, type (rough or final), any areas that could not be cleaned due to ongoing work
handoffs.impact: Supervisao cannot schedule inspection or walkthrough until cleaning is confirmed complete.

handoffs.data_exchanged: Service type, labor hours, materials used, equipment costs, total cost per house
handoffs.impact: Core Craft costs must be tracked per house for accurate project costing. Undefined values (PP-24) make cost tracking unreliable.

handoffs.data_exchanged: Emergency service request via WhatsApp: fallen tree, flooding, debris blocking access, safety hazard
handoffs.impact: Urgent issues block all construction activity until resolved. Response time directly affects construction schedule.

pain_points.description: CUT service is frequently requested without specifying REMOVAL. CUT only cuts/clears vegetation or material but does NOT remove it from site. Supervisors and schedulers assume removal is included, leading to lots with cut vegetation piled on site blocking construction.
pain_points.current_workaround: Yuri or Sales must clarify with requester every time CUT is requested; sometimes crews arrive to find previous CUT debris still on lot from months ago
pain_points.proposed_solution: Service catalog explicitly separates CUT and REMOVAL as two services; CUT request auto-prompts 'Do you also need REMOVAL?'; bundle pricing option for CUT+REMOVAL

pain_points.description: Supervisors bypass daily logs and communicate service requests only via WhatsApp, creating no paper trail and making it impossible to track service history or costs per house
pain_points.current_workaround: Yuri manually creates service records based on WhatsApp conversation threads; some services are never formally recorded
pain_points.proposed_solution: WhatsApp integration that auto-creates service requests from structured messages; or Slack-first policy enforcement with WhatsApp reserved only for true emergencies

pain_points.description: Undefined values for non-standard services: many requests fall outside the standard service catalog and have no established pricing, leading to inconsistent billing and cost overruns
pain_points.current_workaround: Yuri estimates pricing on the fly based on his experience; estimates are not documented and vary by situation
pain_points.proposed_solution: Pricing formula builder for non-standard services based on labor hours, equipment type, material cost, and complexity multiplier; historical pricing database for reference

pain_points.description: No formal daily log compliance from Core Craft crews; work performed is often undocumented, making it difficult to verify what was done, when, and at what cost
pain_points.current_workaround: Sales tracks crew activities informally; end-of-day summary via Slack channel that may or may not be complete
pain_points.proposed_solution: Mobile daily log for crews with simple photo-based completion documentation; GPS-based check-in/check-out at job sites; auto-generated daily summary from check-in data

pain_points.description: Service interdependencies are not tracked in the system; Core Craft must manually coordinate with Scheduling to understand which services are blocking construction activities
pain_points.current_workaround: Yuri and Sales maintain mental model of which services are blocking; sometimes discover too late that a service was needed before a scheduled activity
pain_points.proposed_solution: Integration with Scheduling that auto-flags Core Craft services as prerequisites for construction activities; priority boost when service is on critical path

tribal_knowledge.description: Yuri has developed mental pricing models over years of experience. He can estimate cost for unusual lot clearing (oversized lots, heavily wooded, wetland adjacent), specialty grading (drainage issues, retaining wall prep), and complex handyman repairs. These estimates consider factors like equipment wear, fuel costs, disposal fees, and crew fatigue that are not in any price table.

Yuri and Sales know which services must be sequenced (grading before irrigation, irrigation before sod, rough cleaning before inspection, CUT before grade) and which can be parallelized. They also know the optimal timing for each service relative to construction phase (e.g., dumpster rotation should happen at framing completion, not during framing).

Not all crew members can do all services. Yuri knows the exact capabilities of each person: Joao Marcos can handle electrical punch items but not plumbing; Mateus can do irrigation but also fills in for grading; Natanael is the most versatile substitute but needs supervision for irrigation work. This mapping exists only in Yuri's head.

Yuri tracks equipment maintenance needs mentally. He knows the skid steer needs hydraulic fluid check every 50 hours, the dump trailer axle needs greasing every 2 weeks, and the pressure washer pump fails if run dry for more than 30 seconds. No maintenance log or schedule exists.

Different communities have different standards for landscaping, cleaning, and site maintenance. Some HOAs require specific grass types, tree species, or mulch colors. Some communities have restricted access hours for heavy equipment. Sales knows these requirements from experience.

Yuri knows which disposal sites accept which materials (green waste, construction debris, fill dirt), their current pricing, weight limits, and operating hours. He routes disposal runs to minimize cost and travel time. Some disposal sites have informal relationships that provide better rates.

topics: Dumpster: placement rules, rotation triggers, rental tracking
Site Prep/Materials: staging, scaffold, temporary structures
Garden: landscaping, irrigation, sod, final grade
Cleaning: rough cleaning vs final cleaning standards
Garage: door installation, adjustment, common issues
Handymen: scope limits, when to escalate to licensed trade
Response time expectations per tag type
Definition of CUT scope (what it includes and excludes)
Pricing differences between CUT and REMOVAL
Why daily logs matter (cost tracking, legal, quality)
How to complete a mobile daily log
Time tracking per service per house
When to report equipment issues vs attempt repair

training_curriculum.module: Service Interdependencies and Construction Phase Awareness
topics: Which Core Craft services are prerequisites for construction activities
How to read a construction schedule for priority determination
Critical path awareness for service prioritization
Coordination with Scheduling and Supervisao departments

compliance_requirements.requirement: All lot clearing must comply with environmental regulations regarding protected trees and wetland buffers
compliance_requirements.verification: Verify lot clearing plan against county environmental overlay map before CUT/GRADE operations; document tree removal with species and diameter

compliance_requirements.requirement: Dumpster placement must not obstruct fire lanes, public sidewalks, or utility access
compliance_requirements.verification: Verify placement location against site plan showing fire lanes and utility easements; photograph placement for documentation

compliance_requirements.requirement: Irrigation system installation must comply with local water authority regulations
compliance_requirements.verification: Irrigation plan must include rain sensor (required by Florida statute), proper backflow prevention, and compliance with watering restrictions

compliance_requirements.requirement: Construction debris disposal must use approved disposal facilities
compliance_requirements.verification: Retain disposal receipts showing facility name, date, material type, and weight/volume for each disposal run

compliance_requirements.requirement: Landscaping must meet community HOA requirements and county code for minimum plantings
compliance_requirements.verification: Verify landscape plan meets minimum tree, shrub, and ground cover requirements per county code and HOA standards

compliance_requirements.requirement: Equipment operators must have appropriate licenses and training
compliance_requirements.verification: Maintain training records and certifications for equipment operators; verify currency before assignment

key_metrics.target: Standard services: <2 business days; Urgent: same day
key_metrics.measurement: Time between service request creation in Slack and completion confirmation, tracked by service group

key_metrics.target: 0 construction activities delayed due to pending Core Craft service
key_metrics.measurement: Count of scheduling delays attributed to incomplete Core Craft services; tracked via Scheduling handoff data

key_metrics.target: <5% of CUT requests require follow-up clarification about REMOVAL
key_metrics.measurement: Count of CUT requests requiring clarification divided by total CUT requests

key_metrics.target: 100% of services performed have corresponding daily log entries
key_metrics.measurement: Services completed vs daily log entries with matching house ID and date

key_metrics.target: >80% of available crew hours assigned to revenue-generating work
key_metrics.measurement: Assigned service hours divided by total available crew hours per week

key_metrics.target: >90% of final cleanings accepted without re-clean request
key_metrics.measurement: Final cleanings accepted by Supervisao on first pass divided by total final cleanings performed

key_metrics.target: <5% of scheduled equipment hours lost to breakdowns
key_metrics.measurement: Hours of equipment downtime due to unplanned maintenance divided by total scheduled equipment hours
